The 'Ndrangheta acts in close cooperation with Albanian mafia families in moving weapons and narcotics across Europe.
'Ndrangheta and Camorra both use the UK for money laundering and have investments in London, indicating collaborative financial activities.
The 'Ndrangheta engages in business with local Galician mafias in Spain for drug trafficking.
There are links between the 'Ndrangheta and the Gambino family, with the 'Ndrangheta using New York as a training ground for recruits.
The 'Ndrangheta works in conjunction with the Mexican drug cartel Los Zetas in the drug trade.
The 'Ndrangheta has an alliance with the PCC since the mid-2010s for cocaine trafficking from Brazil to Europe.
The 'Ndrangheta had contacts with the Sicilian Mafia during its expansion and the Sicilian Mafia mediated the end of the Second 'Ndrangheta war.
The gangs had a rivalry that escalated in 2009, involving gang violence and crossfire incidents, with both gangs implicated in 38 homicides between 2009 and 2012.
The 110ers Gang is listed as having a rivalry with the 39ers gang, though the text does not provide specific details about the nature or timeframe of this conflict.
The 110ers Gang has been known to feud with the Young Melph Mafia, leading to bloodshed that lasted for years until federal investigations intervened.
